---
title: "NZ Migration: What the Data Shows"
subtitle: "Monthly migration trends tracked with Statistics NZ data — built by [Autonomous Econ](https://autonomousecon.substack.com)"
---

## The Kiwi Exodus

Net NZ citizen migration peaked at +24,572 in the 12 months to October 2020 while the border was closed and departures collapsed. By March 2026 it stood at -36,538. Over the same 12 months, non-NZ citizens (including Australians) recorded a net +60,787 — down from the post-reopening peak of +176,489 in October 2023.

```{=html}
{{< include data/kiwi-exodus_main.html >}}
```

<p class="data-source">Source: Statistics NZ ITM552301 — rolling 12-month sum.</p>

## Age Profile of Migration

Net migration is dominated by people in their 20s and 30s throughout the series. The post-2022 cycle saw those cohorts reach levels far above anything in the pre-COVID baseline before the recent slowdown.

```{=html}
{{< include data/kiwi-exodus_age_net.html >}}
```

<p class="data-source">Source: Statistics NZ ITM552101 — rolling 12-month net migration (arrivals minus departures), all citizenships.</p>

## India's Rising Share

The composition of net migration has shifted markedly since 2022. India and China now dominate inflows, while the "Other" category has compressed as migration becomes more concentrated.

```{=html}
{{< include data/india-surge_net_area.html >}}
```

<p class="data-source">Source: Statistics NZ ITM552301 — rolling 12-month net migration (arrivals minus departures), non-NZ citizens. China, India, Philippines, Sri Lanka, UK, Fiji plus Other.</p>

The share chart shows each country's arrivals as a proportion of all non-NZ arrivals on a 12-month rolling average.

```{=html}
{{< include data/india-surge_share.html >}}
```

<p class="data-source">Source: Statistics NZ ITM552301 — rolling 12-month average.</p>

Peters specifically singles out the skill level of Indian arrivals. The chart below shows how India's work visa intake has shifted from high-skill toward lower-skill roles since the AEWV launched in mid-2022.

```{=html}
{{< include data/india-surge_skill_shift.html >}}
```

<p class="data-source">Source: MBIE Migration Data Explorer — W3 Work Occupations, approved work visas by nationality and ANZSCO skill level (12-month periods ended May 2017–2026).</p>

India's lower-skill share (36%) is above countries like the UK (16%) and China (11%) — but Vietnam (49%) and Fiji (48%) rank higher. The pattern is not India-specific.

```{=html}
{{< include data/india-surge_country_skill.html >}}
```

<p class="data-source">Source: MBIE Migration Data Explorer — W3 Work Occupations, year ended May 2026. Top 10 source countries by approved work visa volume.</p>

## The Visa Mix Shift

Student and work visa arrivals surged after the border reopened in 2022. The second chart shows the same breakdown for arrivals whose last permanent residence was India.

```{=html}
{{< include data/visa-shift_overall.html >}}
```

<p class="data-source">Source: Statistics NZ ITM552201 — monthly arrivals by visa type.</p>

```{=html}
{{< include data/visa-shift_india_clpr.html >}}
```

<p class="data-source">Source: Statistics NZ ITM553001 — arrivals where Country of Last Permanent Residence = India.</p>

```{=html}
{{< include data/visa-shift_china_clpr.html >}}
```

<p class="data-source">Source: Statistics NZ ITM553001 — arrivals where Country of Last Permanent Residence = China.</p>

## Where They Land

International migration is concentrated in a handful of Territorial Authorities. Auckland and Hamilton lead by absolute numbers, but smaller TAs like Queenstown-Lakes rank higher on a per-capita basis. The map shows cumulative net international migration per 1,000 residents over the three years ended June 2025 — teal indicates net inflow, red indicates net outflow.

```{=html}
{{< include data/where-they-land_map.html >}}
```

<p class="data-source">Source: Stats NZ, Subnational population estimates at 30 June 2025 (provisional, 2025 boundaries). Net international migration = sum of annual net flows, years ended June 2023, 2024 and 2025. Rate per 1,000 = cumulative net ÷ 2025 population × 1,000.</p>

The top 5 TAs by per-capita inflow, and the top 5 Auckland local board areas by per-capita net international migration.

```{=html}
{{< include data/where-they-land_top_inflow.html >}}
```

<p class="data-source">Source: Stats NZ, Subnational population estimates at 30 June 2025 (provisional, 2025 boundaries).</p>

```{=html}
{{< include data/where-they-land_auckland_albs.html >}}
```

<p class="data-source">Source: Stats NZ, Subnational population estimates at 30 June 2025 (provisional, 2025 boundaries).</p>

<p class="data-source">
Built by <a href="https://autonomousecon.substack.com">Autonomous Econ</a> |
<a href="https://github.com/martingeew/nz_migration_streamlit">Source code</a>
</p>
